cot_content:
Okay, so the user is asking how to lock a tape measure. Let me think about this. I know that tape measures have a locking mechanism to hold the tape in place once you've extended it to the desired length.

The options given are A and B. Option A says to press the front locking tab down until it locks, and B says to pull it up. From what I remember, most tape measures have a small button or tab near the end of the tape. When you extend the tape to the length you need, you usually press that tab down to lock it in place. Pressing down would engage the locking mechanism, holding the tape so it doesn't retract. If you pull it up, I'm not sure how that would work. Maybe some models have different mechanisms, but the standard one I've seen is pressing down. So the answer should be A. Let me double-check this. If you press down, the tab clicks into place, securing the tape. Yeah, that makes sense. So the correct answer is A.
label: majority
